Grid.TextBlock:
The Director of Central Intelligence
Washington D.C., 20505

JCS WASHINGTON DC//J3//
USCINCPAC HONOLULU HI
CINCPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI
COMSUBPAC PEARL HARBOR HI

TextBlock.Text: Soviet Coercion of Indonesia and ADMIRAL SENYAVIN SAG

StackPanel.TextBlock:
1. Despite Allied success blunting Soviet attacks in the Indian Ocean, the Indonesian government remains in disarray. Intercepts show Moscow increasing pressure on Indonesian military and cabinet figures to accept neutrality. Soviet demands include denial of Allied basing and transit through Indonesian-controlled straits.
2. Extracted tasking orders Soviet gun cruiser ADMIRAL SENYAVIN to remain north of Jakarta. SAG is directed to demonstrate naval gunfire reach against Tanjung Priok, Jakarta waterfront targets, and coastal infrastructure. It is also directed to interdict shipping assessed to support Allied movement. Command language stresses avoidance of decisive engagement until political objectives are achieved.
3. SENYAVIN force is assessed to have long-range bomber cover from Cam Ranh. Soviet aircraft can reach the Java Sea with reduced interception risk after the effective destruction of Singapore and Malaysian air forces. RMAF Butterworth is assessed out of action after repeated conventional strikes against runways, fuel storage, and maintenance areas.
4. Soviet diplomatic and intelligence channels are offering neutrality guarantees while threatening sustained naval bombardment, fuel blockade, and support to local factions if Jakarta resumes Allied cooperation. The threat of naval gunfire against port and government targets is assessed as central to the coercion effort. The key demand is denial of Indonesian airfields and straits to Allied forces.
5. We judge SENYAVIN force to be the visible arm of this coercion campaign. Destruction or withdrawal of the SAG would improve prospects for Indonesian reentry on the Allied side. This intercept should be considered actionable.

Grid.TextBlock:
Distribution by ER/21 August 1985 w/Att
Orig - Each Addressee
1 - DCI
1 - DDCI
1 - EXDIR
1 - C/NIC
1 - VC/NIC
1 - NIO/Warning
1 - ER File
